meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Idea Validation: Solve Debugging Struggles for AI Students
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to validate your AI ideas first, ensuring you build what works. Master Antigravity and Claude Code for success.
content.answer_block: Debugging frustration often stems from building unvalidated features that lack clear requirements. By validating your core idea first, you ensure your architecture is sound before writing code. Use Antigravity for structural planning and Claude Code for iterative refinement, turning complex errors into learning opportunities rather than insurmountable roadblocks.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, the console flooded with cryptic red error messages. You’ve spent hours chasing a bug that feels like a ghost in the machine, and your motivation is rapidly evaporating. This is the 'valley of despair' every senior engineer once walked, but it doesn't have to be your permanent address.

True mastery isn't about memorizing syntax; it's about validating your logic before you commit to the code. When you align your product vision with robust, validated architecture, debugging shifts from a frantic fire-drill to a surgical process of verification.

steps.title: Define the Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, document exactly what your feature solves. If you can't explain it simply, you can't code it effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product architect. Review my feature scope: [Insert Scope]. Identify three potential failure points in the logic before I begin implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project requirements and identify potential logic gaps or architectural blind spots.'

steps.title: Map the Data Flow
steps.description: Visualize how data moves through your application to identify where bugs are likely to hide.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mermaid.js sequence diagram for the data flow of [Feature]. Highlight potential bottlenecks where asynchronous calls might fail.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a flowchart representing the data state transitions for this feature.'

steps.title: Generate Test-Driven Scaffolding
steps.description: Don't build in the dark. Use automated tests to define success before you start the implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for [Component] that covers edge cases and null pointer exceptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a test file for the current module and ensure 80% branch coverage.'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Build in small, verifiable chunks. If a bug appears, you know exactly which 10 lines caused it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this code block into smaller, pure functions to improve testability and reduce side effects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix 'Analyze the last error trace and provide a patch that addresses the root cause, not the symptom.'

steps.title: Final Validation & Review
steps.description: Perform a final audit to ensure the code meets the validated requirements set in step one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on this implementation. Does it meet the original requirements? Check for security vulnerabilities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review 'Audit the current codebase against best practices for performance and security.'

pro_tips: Always build your error handlers before your logic; it makes the debugging process 10x faster.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude—explain the bug to it, and 50% of the time you'll find the answer yourself.
Focus on 'Pure Functions'—if a function doesn't rely on external state, it is nearly impossible to break.
Never suppress a warning; warnings are just bugs that haven't happened yet.
Keep your git commits atomic—if you break the build, you only have to roll back 5 minutes of work.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully validated and built your first module, deploy it to a staging environment to observe real-world performance, then iterate based on user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the expert guidance to stop guessing and start building production-grade software. The only thing missing is your contribution to the ecosystem. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
